description: Shortly after I pulled up to pond, which is 100 meters+ from the road, I saw a pale tern flying over yonder pond. I assumed it was a FOTE, but as I put my scope on it, I could see its bouncy flight despite little or no wind. I then truly looked at the bird. Crown black. Mantle pale and pearly with a narrow dusky trailing edge to the primaries and paler secondaries. Though pale, there was none of the frosty white look on the primaries that FOTEs have. The tail looked long and deeply forked, head squat and bill smallish, all darkish red, slim (shape as compared to FOTE or COTE). The underparts were notably gray, paler (whitish) on auriculars, imparting a white cheeked look. Underwing white with narrow blackish trailing edge to primaries. As I was watching this bird, a second pale bird flew by. It fit precisely the same description. I couldn't tell the two birds apart.
